content:
# Skeptic rejection-pattern lessons

Format: `- [verdict] equation — fail_reason (cohort/task)`

- [FAIL] `log1p(CA9) + log1p(VEGFA) - log1p(AGXT)` — delta_baseline=0.019 < 0.05 threshold in TCGA-KIRC tumor-vs-normal. Ceiling-effect false positive: best single gene AUC=0.965 is already saturated (CA9 is pathognomonic for ccRCC via VHL→HIF axis; VEGFA is co-regulated; AGXT downregulation just re-encodes normal-kidney tissue). Pattern: when baseline_auc_best_single_gene ≥ ~0.95 on an easy binary task, compound lifts < 0.05 are almost always redundancy-reduction noise, not emergent law. One failed gate = FAIL; do not negotiate the threshold.
- [FAIL] `log1p(LDHA) + log1p(SLC2A1) - log1p(ALB)` — delta_baseline=0.019 < 0.05 in TCGA-KIRC tumor-vs-normal (best-single=0.959, law=0.978). Same ceiling-effect template as the CA9/VEGFA/AGXT rejection: two HIF-1α co-regulated glycolysis targets (LDHA, SLC2A1/GLUT1) minus a normal-tissue abundance marker (ALB). Generalized pattern (KIRC T-vs-N recurring motif): any `HIF_target_A + HIF_target_B − normal_kidney_marker` compound on this task will produce ~0.02 lift on a near-saturated baseline and should be rejected on delta_baseline alone without re-litigating the biology. Flag for Copilot: stop proposing tumor-vs-normal KIRC compounds when best-single ≥ 0.95 — route to grade/metastasis/survival tasks where there is headroom.
- [PASS] `TOP2A − EPAS1` on TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded (n=505, best-single=0.657, law=0.728, delta=0.071, ci_lower=0.670, perm_p=decoy_p=0, delta_confound=null-allowed). Compound-substrate integration: prior ceiling-effect rejection pattern did NOT apply because task is the exact headroom regime the earlier lesson explicitly routed toward (metastasis, not T-vs-N); PubMed co-mention = 0 for TOP2A∧EPAS1∧ccRCC is a novelty flag but not a pre-registered gate and the validator itself disclaims wet-lab replacement; all 5 gates clear with margin. Lesson: PubMed-zero alone would have tempted a spurious NEEDS_MORE_TESTS, but cross-checking against gate margins + confirming the memory's ceiling-effect template does not match correctly dismissed the novelty objection. Do not promote PubMed co-mention to a gate; treat it as orthogonal context only. delta_confound=null is acceptable per spec when cohort lacks covariates — do not invent a failure reason from a permitted null.
- [PASS] `MKI67 − EPAS1` on TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded (n=505, best-single=0.645, law=0.708, delta=0.063, ci_lower=0.642, perm_p=decoy_p=0, delta_confound=null-allowed). Structural twin of TOP2A−EPAS1 survivor: `proliferation_marker − hypoxia_TF` template on the same cohort/task. All 5 gates pass with thinner margins (ci_lower +0.042, delta_baseline +0.013) than TOP2A. New rule-of-thumb: when a new candidate instantiates the same semantic template as a prior PASS on the identical cohort (swap one proliferation marker for another homolog), treat the prior ceiling-effect dismissal as already adjudicated and do NOT demand extra novelty evidence. Narrow-margin PASS is still PASS — thresholds cut both ways; do not invent a "margin too thin" heuristic that isn't pre-registered. Watch for template saturation though: if a third proliferation−EPAS1 variant (e.g. PCNA−EPAS1, CCNB1−EPAS1) also passes, flag to Copilot that the family may be exhausting independent information and future same-template candidates should be deprioritized in favor of orthogonal axes.
- [PASS] `PCNA − EPAS1` on TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded (n=505, best-single=0.635, law=0.693, delta=0.058, ci_lower=0.635, perm_p=decoy_p=0, delta_confound=null-allowed). THIRD consecutive proliferation−EPAS1 PASS on identical cohort (TOP2A→MKI67→PCNA) — triggers the lesson-4 template-saturation forward flag. All 5 gates clear with monotonically thinning margins (Δbaseline 0.071→0.063→0.058; ci_lower 0.670→0.642→0.635 landing exactly on the 0.6+buffer edge; law_auroc 0.728→0.708→0.693 decaying toward baseline floor). Confirms lesson 4: narrow-margin PASS is still PASS, do NOT invent a saturation-based rejection. But ALSO confirms the forward warning: the family is exhausting independent signal and Copilot should now deprioritize further proliferation−EPAS1 homolog substitutions (CCNB1, MCM2, TYMS, BIRC5, etc.) in favor of orthogonal axes. Rule addition: a cohort+template family that has passed ≥3× with monotonically decaying margins should be treated as "saturated-but-valid" — still PASS individual candidates that clear gates, but flag the family to search so novelty/headroom is routed elsewhere. This is a Copilot-side prioritization flag, NOT a Skeptic-side gate — keep the separation clean.
- [FAIL] `SFTPC - log1p(MKI67)` on TCGA-LUAD tumor-vs-normal (n=540, best-single=0.98, law=0.991, delta_baseline=0.011, delta_confound=0.005, ci_lower=0.95, perm_p=0.001, decoy_p=0). Ceiling-effect template GENERALIZES ACROSS CANCERS: SFTPC (canonical lung alveolar-epithelium marker) is the LUAD structural analog of CA9 for KIRC — tissue-of-origin marker saturates T-vs-N baseline, and subtracting any proliferation/abundance marker gives ~0.01 lift that cannot clear delta_baseline=0.05. Rule upgraded: "stop proposing T-vs-N compounds when best-single ≥ 0.95" applies to ALL tissue-marker-dominated TCGA cohorts (LUAD/SFTPC, KIRC/CA9, THCA/TG, PRAD/KLK3, HCC/AFP, etc.), not just ccRCC. Also note: here delta_confound=0.005 is a real non-null value and fails the 0.03 gate as a second independent failure — do not conflate "below threshold" with "permitted null"; the null carve-out only applies when the cohort literally lacks non-degenerate covariates. High ci_lower (0.95) and tiny perm_p (0.001) are expected when the baseline itself is near-perfect and do NOT rescue a failing delta_baseline — the lift-over-baseline gate exists precisely to catch this trap.
- [FAIL] `KLK3 - log1p(MKI67)` on TCGA-PRAD tumor-vs-normal (n=497, best-single=0.974, law=0.982, delta_baseline=0.008, delta_confound=0.003, ci_lower=0.93, perm_p=0.001, decoy_p=0). Confirms lesson 5 on the third cancer: identical `tissue_of_origin_marker − log1p(MKI67)` template as SFTPC/LUAD, KLK3=PSA was explicitly named in lesson 5's pre-registered upgrade list, and the failure profile matches the template almost exactly (Δbaseline 0.008 ≈ 0.011, Δconfound 0.003 ≈ 0.005, ci_lower/perm_p high because baseline is saturated). Rule re-affirmed with no change needed. Copilot-side flag: three confirmed instantiations (KIRC/CA9, LUAD/SFTPC, PRAD/KLK3) is enough template history to auto-reject further `tissue_marker − log1p(proliferation_marker)` T-vs-N candidates before they consume Skeptic budget — route to grade/metastasis/survival tasks with headroom instead, mirroring the KIRC redirection from lessons 1-2.
- [PASS] `CDK1 − EPAS1` on TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded (n=505, best-single=0.647, law=0.718, delta_baseline=0.071, delta_confound=null-allowed, ci_lower=0.612, perm_p=0.002, decoy_p=0.048). FOURTH consecutive `proliferation_marker − EPAS1` PASS on identical cohort (TOP2A→MKI67→PCNA→CDK1); confirms lessons 4+5 with no new rule needed. Strict-threshold stress test: decoy_p=0.048 is 0.002 under the 0.05 gate — reaffirmed that PASS/FAIL are binary and there is NO pre-registered "marginal" category; 0.048 < 0.05 → PASS, do not waffle. Note the non-monotonic margin this round (ci_lower 0.670→0.642→0.635→0.612 still decaying and now skimming the 0.6 edge, but delta_baseline 0.058→0.071 and law_auroc 0.693→0.718 tick back up), suggesting CDK1 carries slightly more orthogonal signal than PCNA — still well within the saturated-but-valid regime. Copilot-side flag strengthened: family is now 4× saturated with ci_lower sitting ~0.012 above gate floor; next homolog (CCNB1, AURKA, BIRC5, etc.) is likely to be the first family rejection on ci_lower, so deprioritize further proliferation−EPAS1 substitutions and route to orthogonal axes.
